Decision: relax referent-free generation for an MVP

Referent stripping in generation (system prompt and filter) is temporarily switched off so the pipeline produces a functional, knowledgeable prompt-response model, since other work depends on having a working small language model. Referent-free generation is deferred, to return through a constructed world-state generator rather than through prompt and filter restriction.

The generation system prompt (prompts.py) no longer forbids real facts, named entities, numbers, dates, or technical vocabulary; the filter (filters.py) no longer blocklists real-world words or bans digits. Only assistant-framing and meta-commentary detection remain, since those are corpus-quality problems independent of referent-freedom. Severity (s1 versus s2) still selects concrete-noun versus category-level prompt vocabulary, but no longer changes what content is allowed.

This decision reprioritizes G1 (node 1) below a practical milestone: a working, trainable, evaluable prompt-response model, since multiple other elements of the project are blocked on having one to test against. It is explicitly temporary and does not retract the referent-free premise; the plan is to reintroduce referent-freeness once the MVP has validated the training and evaluation pipeline end to end, and to do so through the persistent world-state and program-as-author architecture (node 36, node 38) rather than by re-tightening prompts.py and filters.py directly, since the earlier prompt-and-filter approach is the mechanism that produced the diversity-contraction and consistency problems (node 34, node 35) in the first place. The definition text type was also changed in this pass from inventing headwords to defining real words, so the model acquires genuine lexical knowledge during this MVP phase.
